title: Classify and contain stray /tmp Git marker
goal: Determine the provenance and live ownership of the unexplained /tmp/.git marker, then remove, relocate or contain it only if safe, and make temporary-repository tests robust against unrelated ancestor Git markers without weakening real worktree guards.

acceptance.assertion: The /tmp/.git marker is classified with owner/provenance and current process/task/worktree dependencies before any effect.
No marker is deleted or changed while it may belong to an active or foreign workflow; uncertainty fails closed.
If safe, stale host residue is removed through a bounded auditable path; otherwise Steuerboard tests are isolated or hardened without weakening production worktree detection.
Steuerboard deploy-check passes in the normal host environment and a synthetic ancestor-marker case proves the intended temporary-repository behavior.

metadata.source_finding: A host-level /tmp/.git directory dated 2026-07-12 caused 22 Steuerboard tests to misclassify /tmp/test-repo as inside a Git worktree; a clean-/tmp Bubblewrap run passed all 1,453 tests.
does_not_establish: that it is safe to delete
that Steuerboard production worktree detection is incorrect
